courses.name: Democracy in Theory and Practice
courses.description: Introduction to modern political ideas such as liberty, equality, the rule of law, representation, participation (including gender aspects), the impact of these ideas on political and policy making institutions in Canada; other countries may be examined. Basic research and academic writing skills.

courses.name: Politics in the World
courses.description: Compares politics in selected states and world regions, including political institutions and cultures, development, public policy making, and gender. Global issues and international relations among states, international organizations, and other actors. Basic research and academic writing skills.

courses.name: Technology, Nature, Power
courses.description: Social media, self-driving cars, genetic manipulation: technology is transforming both the human experience and the natural world. This course explores interactions among technological change, the evolution of social and political order, and the transformation of the environment (for example, with climate change).

courses.name: Politics of Migration
courses.description: Introduction to concepts and theories that help explain the complex phenomenon of human migration, including the social and political relevance of different types of migration to Canada and in other regions and the political responses to migration and mobility today.

courses.name: Canadian Politics and Society
courses.description: An examination of the cultural, social, and economic context of Canadian politics, including interest groups and social movements, regionalism, language, ethnicity, and gender.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Institutions and Power in Canadian Politics
courses.description: An examination of Canadian political institutions, including federalism, Parliament, the constitution, political parties and the electoral system.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Comparative Politics of the Global North
courses.description: Domestic politics in states of the Global North. Comparison of political and economic regimes, political institutions, actors, political processes and cultures, and patterns of public policy making.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Comparative Politics of the Global South
courses.description: Introduction to domestic politics in post-colonial and developing states of the Global South. Topics may include nationalism, authoritarianism, economic development, revolution, democratization, and the politics of gender, religion, and ethnicity.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Introduction to U.S. Politics
courses.description: An examination of several important aspects of the U.S. political system, including separation of powers, checks and balances, and federalism.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: History of Political Thought I
courses.description: Study of the foundations of democracy, law, and political regimes, within a broader reflection on virtue and the good life in Western classical political thought. Course may include texts by Sophocles, Thucydides, Plato, Aristotle, Augustine, Aquinas, de Pizan, and others.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: History of Political Thought II
courses.description: Study of the emergence, transformations, uses, and meanings of modern political concepts such as liberty, legitimacy, equality, rights, sovereignty, authority, and the state through the interpretation of Western political thinkers such as Machiavelli, Hobbes, Locke, Rousseau, Hume, Wollstonecraft, Marx, Mill and others.
courses.prerequisites: PSCI 2301 or permission of the department.

courses.name: Public Affairs Analysis
courses.description: Introduction to central concepts and processes involved in public affairs. Exploration of public issues, policy approaches and decision-making structures using theoretical, empirical and applied approaches.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Gender and Politics
courses.description: Introduction to gender and politics of diversity, including how feminist activism and organizing finds expression in the political process and structures of representation such as political parties, legislatures and the state.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: International Relations: Global Politics
courses.description: Introduction to theories, concepts and issues in global politics. Topics may include conflict and intervention, peace and security, international institutions, norms and ethics, human rights, gender, culture, and globalization.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: International Relations: Global Political Economy
courses.description: Introduction to the international political economy. Topics may include contemporary changes in the global political economy, multinational corporations, foreign economic policy, global and regional economic institutions, environmental issues, international development and relations between rich and poor countries.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: How to Do Research in Political Science
courses.description: This course focuses on key elements of the research process, including how to ask questions and find answers using ethically informed research design. Students learn to develop a research proposal, and how to critically analyze and write evidence-informed arguments.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: A Statistical Toolkit for Political Scientists
courses.description: The interpretation and application of statistical techniques for data analysis in the study of politics.
courses.prerequisites: PSCI 2701 or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Political Parties and Elections in Canada
courses.description: The evolution of the party system, the growth of major and minor party movements and the electoral process in Canada.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Ontario Government and Politics
courses.description: A survey of the political process and political institutions in Ontario.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Social Power in Canadian Politics
courses.description: The role of social forces in the Canadian political process, including interest groups, social movements, elites and classes.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Constitutional Politics in Canada
courses.description: The politics of the Canadian constitution. Particular attention to historical and contemporary constitutional reform.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Indigenous Politics of Turtle Island
courses.description: Indigenous lived experience in North America, understanding that Indigenous people are active political agents influencing policies and narratives. The course is organized around case studies focused on the social determinants of health, such as income, housing, and social inclusion.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Politics of Development in Africa
courses.description: The historical background of African independence, and contemporary struggle for democracy and economic development in Africa.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Conflict and Security in Africa
courses.description: African conflict and security dynamics, analyzing civil war, communal disputes, and political violence. Topics include state fragility, climate change's impact, human rights, gender dimensions in peacebuilding, along with regional and international responses to conflict and insecurity.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Politics of Development of China
courses.description: The evolving structures and processes of government in (greater) China with particular emphasis on politics in the People's Republic of China and secondary emphasis on Taiwan and Hong Kong.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: State, Society and Economy in Northeast Asia
courses.description: The relationship between government structures, society and the economy in Northeast Asia with particular emphasis on Japan and Korea.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Imperialism and Decolonization
courses.description: Ideologies and practices of European/Western efforts to control Asia, Africa, and Latin America and resistance to them. Topics include the complexities of imperial control and colonial relationships, race and racism, economic impacts, and decolonization.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: The Causes of War
courses.description: Alternate theories of the causes of war. Such alternate perspectives as biological, social and comparative historical approaches, including the results of peace research activities of the past two decades.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing and PSCI 2601 .

courses.name: Politics of Popular Culture
courses.description: Examines political themes in popular culture. Cultural media may include film, literature, television, music, cartoons/comics, and the news media. Political themes may include war, ethnicity, nationalism, revolution, citizenship, gender and sexuality.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: The Politics of Law and Morality
courses.description: Politics of moral regulation in Canada, the United States and other jurisdictions. The treatment in law and public policy of such human rights issues as: capital punishment, sexual orientation, euthanasia, abortion, new reproductive technologies, racial discrimination, religious and equality rights.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing and one of PSCI 2002 , PSCI 2003 or PSCI 2101 .

courses.name: China in the Global South
courses.description: China's role in the Global South, analyzing its impact in Africa, the Middle East, Asia, and Latin America through theoretically nuanced and empirically informed comparative analyses as well as the role of local and global responses.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing.

courses.name: U.S. Constitutional Politics
courses.description: The central role played by the U.S. Constitution in the country's political life, from the Framers to current controversies. Includes issues of race, class and gender.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Government and Politics in the Middle East
courses.description: The evolution and functioning of political systems in the Middle East region, with emphasis on the problems of political stability, the impact of the West, the role of Islam, and war and peace.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Politics of Latin America
courses.description: An overview of the evolution of Latin American political systems, including the impact of the European conquest, democratization, economic liberalization, state-civil society relations, gender politics, revolutionary movements, and relations with the United States.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Mexican Politics
courses.description: An introduction to the politics, society and economy of Mexico. Topics include processes of democratization and economic liberalization, human rights, the environment, the role of women, labour, and indigenous peoples, and social policy. Special emphasis on Mexico's role in the North American political economy.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: European Democracies
courses.description: A comparative examination of select controversies over democracy in specific European countries, considered within the context of 20th century historical trends, as well as contemporary political debates.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Politics of the European Union
courses.description: The process of European integration; the European Union and its institutions; core EU policies, challenges to the integration process (e.g. democratic legitimacy, enlargement); theories of European integration.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Politics in Russia and Ukraine: Power and Contestation
courses.description: Political development in post-Soviet Russia and Ukraine, including examination of the complicated relationship between the two states. Historical perspectives, institutional context (including federalism) and comparative insights.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Reconstruction and Transformation in Europe and Eurasia
courses.description: The politics of dramatic political changes, such as revolution, secession, constitutional revision, and systemic reform. The course will include selected historical and comparative cases from Central and Eastern Europe and the former Soviet Union.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Electoral Politics in the U.S.
courses.description: An overview of specific aspects of U.S. electoral politics, including presidential and congressional elections, incumbency, the two-party system, campaign spending limits, the role of the media, and voter turnout.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Politics and Literature
courses.description: A study of imaginative prose in which political ideas and/or political settings dominate. Literature as political communication, the impact of literature upon politics, the peculiar value of literature in the study of politics and its shortcomings.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Feminist Political Theory
courses.description: Introduction to feminist philosophical responses to sexism, taking into consideration the different waves of feminist discourse. Topics may include the concept of gender; women's diversity and its implications; `intersectionality'; gender, capitalism and the family; and new approaches to feminist knowledge and feminist agency.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Politics of Human Rights
courses.description: Politics of human rights in its historical and cultural context, including: early liberal theories of natural rights; utilitarian and Marxist critiques; contemporary rights debates; different generations of rights; feminism and women's rights; cultural relativism; state sovereignty; and, problems of implementation and enforcement.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Modern Ideologies
courses.description: A survey of ideologies, mainly since 1900, including some of nationalism, utopian socialism, communism, fascism, populism, environmentalism and feminism.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Global Indigenous Politics
courses.description: An overview of regional and international Indigenous politics with case studies from the Americas, Europe, Asia, the Pacific; Africa.  Topics include colonization, state formation, decolonial and postcolonial theories, Indigenous movements, the role of the United Nations, land rights, environment, self-determination, development, gender, and sexuality.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: History of Muslim Political Thought
courses.description: A survey of political thought among Muslims, tracing the emergence and influence of juridical, philosophical and administrative approaches to politics on Muslim civilization.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Enlightenment Political Thought
courses.description: Major Enlightenment thinkers and major themes of Enlightenment political thought. Topics may include reason, religion, toleration, liberty, equality, the foundations of political authority, autonomy, morals, taste, progress, history or commerce.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Contemporary Approaches to Political Inquiry
courses.description: Overview of debates around fundamental concepts in the social sciences and how theorists have understood them. Introduction to different paradigms and approaches, which may include positivism, Weberian ideal types, naturalism, pragmatism, social realism, critical rationalism, genealogy, structuralism, phenomenology, hermeneutics, deconstruction, and discourse analysis.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing and ( PSCI 2301 and PSCI 2302 ), or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Canadian Public Policy
courses.description: Policy communities and policy networks in Canada with particular attention paid to policy issues, the political environment, policy instruments, impact and outcomes.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing and one of PSCI 2002 , PSCI 2003 , PSCI 2401 , or PAPM 2000 (no longer offered).

courses.name: Comparative Public Policy Analysis
courses.description: The formation and impact of public policy: a variety of political systems as well as a variety of policy areas. Emphasis on developing skills for the analysis of policy formation and impact.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing and one of PSCI 2101 , PSCI 2401 , or PAPM 2001 and PAPM 2002 .

courses.name: Public Affairs and Media Strategies
courses.description: The public affairs and issue management strategies of corporations, government departments, and other institutions in Canada from a comparative perspective.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Public Opinion and Public Policy
courses.description: Theories about the origins and dynamics of public opinion, the ways in which public opinion influences government policy and decision-making, and how decision-makers are able to shape public opinion.
courses.prerequisites: PSCI 2701 and PSCI 2702 .

courses.name: Introduction to Political Management
courses.description: Introduction to the field of political management. The institutional, legislative and ethical context in which party strategists, campaign managers, pollsters, lobbyists and civil society operate. Related administrative and communications skills.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Data Analysis for Governance: Formal Approaches and Practical Realities
courses.description: Finding and using data to make, manage and evaluate public policy. Emphasis is on developing data analysis skills, and using and applying substantive theories by working on projects with real-world applications.
courses.prerequisites: PSCI 2701 and PSCI 2702 .

courses.name: Gender and Politics: Global South
courses.description: A contemporary approach to the role of gender in political systems of the South. Topics may include gender and development, human rights, social policies, globalization, state-civil society relations, political participation and citizenship.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing.

courses.name: International Institutions
courses.description: Origins, structure and functioning of international institutions with emphasis on the United Nations as well as regional organizations. Topics include peace and security, international aid and development, human rights and the control of global resources.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing and one of PSCI 2601 or PSCI 2602 .

courses.name: Theories of International Politics
courses.description: Examination of the major theoretical approaches to the study of international politics. Topics may include realism, liberalism, Marxism, constructivism, feminism, and poststructuralism.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing and PSCI 2601 .

courses.name: Strategic Thought and International Security
courses.description: The ideas of classical and contemporary strategic thinkers. International security issues and concepts.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Canadian Foreign Policy
courses.description: The traditions, domestic influences, objectives, capabilities, and decision-making processes, and analysis of selected contemporary issues.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing and one of PSCI 2002 , PSCI 2003 , PSCI 2601 or PSCI 2602 .

courses.name: Canadian Defence Policy at Home and Abroad
courses.description: Canadian defence policy as it pertains to Canada, North America, NORAD, the Arctic, NATO/Europe, and the Indo-Pacific.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Migration Governance
courses.description: Advanced introduction to the politics of human mobility and migration, including attempts by states and other actors to influence migration and mobility and emerging types of governance at the national, regional and global levels.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Global Politics of Food
courses.description: Drawing on theories of international relations, political economy, and public policy-making, this course examines the global, national and local politics of food production and distribution. Topics include food security, free trade versus fair trade, the environmental sustainability of food systems, food sovereignty and food aid.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Government and Politics of South Asia
courses.description: Patterns of colonialism, evolving political regimes and issues in development and foreign policy in the countries of South Asia, including India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and other member states of SAARC.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing.

courses.name: The Politics of Israel/Palestine
courses.description: Contested dynamics in and regarding Israel and Palestine, including the development of Zionism, the Nakba, diaspora identities, human rights activism, and debates over analytical frameworks such as settler colonialism, competing nationalisms, and apartheid.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Governing in the Global Economy
courses.description: The main approaches and policy issues in the political economy of advanced industrialized states. The relationship between state and market and the ways in which national states have responded to the pressures of governing in an increasingly interdependent global economy.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing and PSCI 2602 .

courses.name: Environmental Politics
courses.description: Environmental issues in contemporary political argument. Topics include: environmental movements and green parties, environmental ethics and animal rights, economic approaches to environmental management, the politics of sustainable development, and the international politics of the environment.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Globalization and Human Rights
courses.description: An examination of the various dimensions and meanings of globalization and its relationship with human rights. The main emphasis will be on the implications of the emerging global economy for economic, social, political and cultural rights.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing and one of: PSCI 2601 , PSCI 2602 , LAWS 2105 , PHIL 2103 or ( ANTH 1001 and ANTH 1002 ), or ( SOCI 1001 and SOCI 1002 ).

courses.name: Politics of Race
courses.description: The meaning, sources and practice of racialism, as well as efforts to combat it, in a comparative context. Case studies will include South Africa, the United States, and Canada.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Selected Topics in Political Science
courses.description: A lecture course on a selected contemporary topic in Political Science. Topic may vary from year to year and will be announced in advance of the registration period by the Department of Political Science.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Washington Center Internship
courses.description: One-term internship at The Washington Center in D.C.; options in American politics, international affairs, and other areas. Evaluation by Washington Center faculty, but governed by Carleton University Political Science Department regulations. Graded Sat or Uns.
courses.prerequisites: selection to The Washington Center Internship Program and one of PSCI 2200 , PSCI 3200 , or PSCI 3210 and permission of the department.

courses.name: Ottawa Experience Placement, Two Terms
courses.description: The student placement provides an opportunity to work with an organization whose focus relates to politics over a full academic year. Students complete career-related assignments, maintain a placement log and complete a research paper related to their placement.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year Honours standing with a minimum Political Science CGPA of 9.0; successful completion of PSCI 2701 and PSCI 2702 ; or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Ottawa Experience Placement, One Term
courses.description: The student placement provides an opportunity to work with an organization whose focus relates to politics over one academic term. Students complete a career-related assignment, maintain a placement log and complete a research paper related to their placement.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year Honours standing with a minimum Political Science CGPA of 9.0; successful completion of PSCI 2701 and PSCI 2702 ; or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Summer Field Research Course
courses.description: Field course outside of the Ottawa region, potentially outside Canada, with opportunities for research and/or community engagement. A supplementary charge may apply.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Political Science or GPOL, or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Politics and the Media
courses.description: The role of the mass media in the Canadian political system from a comparative perspective.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Sport and Politics
courses.description: A seminar on the relationship between sport and politics, topics covered may include: Canadian sport policy and public administration; sport and social inclusion with a focus on sex, gender, Indigeneity, race, and class; sport and nation-building; sport and social-protest; and, sport and international relations.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the department.

courses.name: Canadian Federalism
courses.description: The evolution and contemporary operation of the Canadian federal system; the social, political, economic, and structural features underlying its operational performance, resilience in crisis, and potential for adaptation.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2003 or PSCI 2101 .

courses.name: Legislatures and Representation in Canada
courses.description: The role of Parliament and of the individual M.P. in terms of policy making, party discipline, and differing conceptions of representation.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: National Security and Intelligence in the Modern State
courses.description: The state's response to foreign espionage, alleged subversion, terrorism, and counterintelligence. Major focus on the Canadian experience, but with extensive use of materials chronicling the practices of KGB, CIA, BIS, ASIO, MOSSAD, etc.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Quebec Politics
courses.description: Society, culture, economy and politics in Quebec. Special attention to the politically relevant changes since 1960 and the central place of Quebec within the Canadian federation.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Executive Power in Canadian Politics
courses.description: Consideration of prime ministers, premiers, cabinet ministers and senior public service leadership in Canadian politics and government.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: The Modern State
courses.description: A survey of recent thinking about the state in western societies drawing on perspectives such as those of feminists, Marxists, Weberians, poststructuralists and others. Topics may include: the rise of the modern state, economic governance, the public sphere, citizenship, sovereignty and territoriality.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Development in the Global South - Theory and Practice
courses.description: Different theoretical approaches to the concept of development in the Global South and their relevance for selected countries in Latin America, Africa and Asia.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Selected Problems in Development in the Global South
courses.description: Topics may include global issues of trade, finance and production, changing patterns of foreign aid, and the role of microfinance, mining, non-governmental organizations, migration, anti-poverty programs and activism in promoting development.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Political Participation in Canada
courses.description: The causes and implications of political participation by individuals with special reference to Canada. Topics include citizen participation in campaign and party organizations, political protest movements, interest groups, and community associations.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2002 , PSCI 2003 , PSCI 2101 , PSCI 2102 , PSCI 2700, or ( PSCI 2701 and PSCI 2702 ).

courses.name: The Politics of the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms
courses.description: The genesis and impact of the Charter of Rights and Freedoms. Particular emphasis on the politics of aboriginal, language, and equality rights.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Southern Africa After Apartheid
courses.description: The pathology of apartheid, the reasons for its end, and prospects for democratization and development in Southern Africa in the era of globalization.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Fighting for Votes
courses.description: Election campaign dynamics and election outcomes, with emphasis on the strategies and actions of voters, parties, and candidates. Attention to concepts of representation, accountability, and legitimacy.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2003 , PSCI 2101 , PSCI 2102 , or ( PSCI 2701 and PSCI 2702 ).

courses.name: Indigenous Activism on Turtle Island: Take that, colonialism!
courses.description: Issues of governance regarding the original peoples of Canada, Mexico and the United States since the European invasion. Contemporary movements for restoration of cultural, political, socio-economic, land and self-governance rights, emphasizing domestic and international strategies.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2002 , PSCI 2003 , PSCI 2101 , PSCI 2102 , PSCI 3013 , or PSCI 3205 .

courses.name: Globalization, Adjustment and Democracy in Africa
courses.description: The nature of global pressures in Africa, as states go through political and economic change.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Westminster Democracies: Parliaments, Parties and Elections
courses.description: Examination of party and parliamentary democracy in the five principal Anglophone parliamentary democracies: Australia, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand and the United Kingdom. Consideration is given to the effects of different electoral systems and institutional arrangements on electoral politics, political participation, and party organization.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2003 , PSCI 2101 , or PSCI 2102 .

courses.name: Political Identity through Graphic Novels
courses.description: Examination of the sources and dynamics of political identity through the medium of graphic novels and graphic memoirs. Themes may include collective memory, genocide, prostitution, violent conflict, civil rights, race and ethnicity, revolution, Indigenous issues, mental health, and gender and sexuality.
courses.prerequisites: fourth year standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Op-Ed Writing and Social Media as Political Engagement
courses.description: The art and craft of political opinion writing and social-media engagement. An examination of contemporary online activism, interpersonal and collective online dynamics, and an imparting of the skills required for persuasive and well-researched op-ed writing.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Political Thought in the Modern Muslim Middle East
courses.description: Contemporary secular and religious responses to the challenges of modernity. Readings include writings of Arab, Turkish, and Iranian intellectuals.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of ( PSCI 2301 and PSCI 2302 ) or PSCI 3311 .

courses.name: Genealogies of Politics and Governance
courses.description: Examination of Foucault’s genealogical method for doing critical studies of politics and governance. Topics may include governmentality, sovereignty, biopolitics, neoliberalism, citizenship, and colonialism.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Political Theories of Democracy and Empire
courses.description: An exploration of how ancient and modern conceptions of empire differ and how the pursuit of empire abroad can undermine good government at home.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing and ( PSCI 2301 and PSCI 2302 ), or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Politics and the Study of History
courses.description: An exploration of the relationship between history and politics. Will examine different forms of history and historical writing, competing conceptions of how to interpret the past, and different accounts of how history is implicated in political judgment and understanding politics.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing.

courses.name: Contemporary Political Theory
courses.description: Examines major currents and themes in continental political thought since the early twentieth century. These may include existentialist, Critical Theory, feminist, and poststructural approaches in relation to topics, such as crises of modernity, (post-) modern reconfigurations of power, and perspectives for democracy.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing and ( PSCI 2301 and PSCI 2302 ) or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Concepts of Political Community I
courses.description: Critical survey of concepts of political community, including the common good, justice, citizenship, leadership, democracy, and legitimacy, from ancient, modern, and contemporary political theory.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Concepts of Political Community II
courses.description: A continued critical survey of concepts of political community, including the common good, justice, citizenship, statesmanship, democracy, and legitimacy, from ancient, modern, and contemporary political theory.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Socio-Technical Change and Public Policy Design
courses.description: Joint implications of contemporary science, technology and demographics for the design of public policy. The main emphasis of the course will be general patterns of change and design relating to public policy.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Reproductive Rights Policy in North America
courses.description: The interaction between social movements, legislatures and courts in formulating reproductive rights policy in Canada, the U.S. and Mexico.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: The Design and Evolution of Public Institutions
courses.description: An examination of the emergence, development and collapse of institutional collective action in a broad historical framework, with attention to probable future scenarios for change.  Readings are taken from anthropology, economics, history and empirical political theory.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Public Policy: Content and Creation
courses.description: The content and creation of public policy. Focus on the explanation, prediction and design of policy. Perspectives and examples are drawn from a variety of frameworks and from both Canadian and non-Canadian contexts.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2401 , PSCI 3402 , PSCI 3405 , PSCI 3409, or PAPM 2001 and PAPM 2002 .

courses.name: Public Affairs Management and Analysis
courses.description: Theories and practice in the management of public affairs, including the environment and administration of the public sector, public opinion, and public communications.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Gender and Globalization
courses.description: How globalization affects women's involvement in politics and how they organize to conceptualize and pursue gender justice in official politics; grass roots projects and cultural transformations; ideology; stand-alone movements; and mixed-sex movements like nationalism and democratization.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2101 , PSCI 2102 , PSCI 2601, PSCI 2602 , PSCI 2500 , PSCI 3500, PSCI 3502 .

courses.name: Politics of Identity in Europe and the Russian Area
courses.description: The relationships between political transformation, identity-building, ethnicity, and gender politics in post-communist states, considered in comparison with select countries in Central and/or Western Europe.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department and one of PSCI 2101, PSCI 2102 , PSCI 2500 , PSCI 3208 , PSCI 3209 , PSCI 3500, PSCI 3502 .

courses.name: Post-Soviet States and Societies
courses.description: The relationship between social forces and state structures at both the national and local levels in the USSR and the post-communist states.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 3208 , PSCI 3209 .

courses.name: Politics of Central Eurasia
courses.description: Examination of the Caucasus and Central Asia, from Chechnya to former Soviet republics of the region, Afghanistan and Chinese Turkestan. Interests of Russia, China, and the United States. Emphasis on underdevelopment, oil and gas, terrorism, Islam.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Politics of the Caucasus and Caspian Basin
courses.description: Examination of the South Caucasus (Azerbaijan, Georgia, Armenia), the Russian-held North Caucasus, including Chechnya, and relations with Iran. Emphasis on state and society, oil and gas, transregional communications, interests of western powers, ethnic relations.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Transitions to Democracy
courses.description: A comparative analysis of processes of democratization. Diverse theoretical approaches to understanding the timing, causes, nature, and limitations of democratization. Examples from Europe and Russia, Latin America, Africa, and Asia.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2102 , PSCI 3100 , PSCI 3204 , PSCI 3208 , PSCI 3209 , PSCI 3500, PSCI 3502 .

courses.name: Women, Power and Political Representation
courses.description: An examination of women’s participation in contemporary electoral politics, including as voters, legislators, and political leaders. Specific attention is given to research on intersectionality.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: The Balkans since 1989
courses.description: Selected topics in Balkan politics and society since the collapse of communism in 1989, focusing on the democratic transition and the EU accession process. The legacies of communist rule, democratization and the many national questions that still exist in the region.
courses.prerequisites: fourth year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Foreign Policies of Soviet Successor States
courses.description: The foreign policies of the USSR and of Russia and selected other successor states, with special emphasis on the search for a new security order.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2102 , PSCI 2601 , PSCI 2602 , PSCI 3107 , PSCI 3208 , PSCI 3209 , PSCI 3600 , PSCI 3603 , PSCI 3703 .

courses.name: Analysis of International Political Economy
courses.description: Various theoretical approaches to the study of the international political economy, with a focus on historical development and changing international structures.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2602 , PSCI 3600 , or PSCI 3703 .

courses.name: Selected Problems in International Political Economy
courses.description: Contemporary problems and issues in the international political economy, with particular attention given to advanced industrial countries.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2602 , PSCI 3600 , or PSCI 3703 .

courses.name: Gender in International Relations
courses.description: Analysis of feminist approaches to international relations. Substantive issues include the role of women in war and militarization, the gender dimensions of global political economy and gender issues in international development.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department and one of PSCI 2601 , PSCI 2602 , PSCI 3500, PSCI 3303 or PSCI 3502 .

courses.name: American Foreign Policy
courses.description: The sources, trends and conflicting interpretations of the international roles of the United States since World War II. Foreign policy machinery and processes assessed in terms of the relative importance of perceptions, ideology, self-interest, and domestic and foreign pressures.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2101 , PSCI 2601 , PSCI 2602 , PSCI 3200 , PSCI 3603 , PSCI 3703 .

courses.name: Politics of North America
courses.description: A seminar examining the evolving relationship between Canada, the United States and Mexico, including political, economic, social, environmental and defence aspects.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: European Integration and European Security
courses.description: A seminar focusing on issues related to the formation of supra-national decision-making structures in Europe.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the department.

courses.name: Selected Topics in European Integration Studies
courses.description: A seminar focusing on selected topics related to European integration in the post-World War II period.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the department.

courses.name: Politics of Migration Management
courses.description: Seminar course that critically engages with innovative policies and instruments under the umbrella of ‘migration management’, and the proliferation of actors (states, international organizations, NGOs, private companies etc) involved in shaping and contributing to migration governance.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the department.

courses.name: Africa’s International Relations
courses.description: Africa's international relations, analyzing interactions between states, the continent and the world, and Africa's diaspora considering history, theories, and current issues. Topics include diplomacy, security, political economy, and global governance.  Includes: Experiential Learning Activity.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing.

courses.name: Capstone Seminar in Global Politics
courses.description: Advanced seminar on a topic in global or comparative politics, applying theories and knowledge gained in previous courses in the Global Politics Specialization.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in BGInS Global Politics Specialization.

courses.name: Intermediate Polimetrics for Micro Data
courses.description: Research designs and statistical techniques primarily used in analyzing survey data. Selected topics may vary from year to year. Students doing Honours papers based on micro data are advised to take this course.
courses.prerequisites: PSCI 2700 or ( PSCI 2701 and PSCI 2702 ), or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Intermediate Research Methods for Applied Political Science
courses.description: Applied methods for policy, politics and public affairs. Primarily quantitative, but may have qualitative elements.
courses.prerequisites: PSCI 2700 or ( PSCI 2701 and PSCI 2702 ), or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Advanced International Relations Theory
courses.description: Close reading and analysis of theoretical research in the academic discipline of International Relations; may include analysis of methodology, normative and critical theory, and key theoretical concepts such as anarchy, sovereignty, power, inequality, coloniality, security, gender.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2601 , PSCI 2602 , PSCI 3601 .

courses.name: Selected Problems in Global Politics
courses.description: The application of international relations theories to specific global problems, both historical and contemporary. Selected issues may focus on one or more of conflict analysis, terrorism, the environment, migration, globalization and global civil society.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2601 , PSCI 2602 , PSCI 3107 , PSCI 3600 , PSCI 3601 , PSCI 3603 , and PSCI 3703 .

courses.name: Foreign Policies of Major East Asian Powers
courses.description: The foreign policies of the East Asian powers, with special attention to China and Japan; an analysis of the domestic sources of policy, capabilities, interests, decision-making processes and foreign relations.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2102 , PSCI 2601 , PSCI 2602 , PSCI 3102 , or PSCI 3103 .

courses.name: Global Money Rules
courses.description: An exploration of the organization of the global monetary and financial system. Issues covered include the relationship between global finance and the state, the politics of world money, and the problems associated with regulating internationally-active financial institutions.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing and one of PSCI 2602 , PSCI 3600 , or PSCI 3703 , or permission of the Department.

courses.name: NATO and World Order
courses.description: NATO as a political and military alliance. NATO and 21st century threats. Security roles for the E.U. Broader translatlantic security issues.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing and one of PSCI 2601 , PSCI 3603 , or PSCI 3607 , or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Politics of Citizenship and Migration
courses.description: How flows of people -- migrants, temporary workers and refugees -- challenge state sovereignty, citizenship and belonging. Emphasis on role of the state, supranational structures and international organizations in migration and mobility.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Global Environmental Politics
courses.description: Global politics of transboundary environmental issues such as biodiversity protection, climate change and desertification. The perspectives, actors, institutions and economic relationships affecting international policy responses to these issues.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department, and one of PSCI 2401 , PSCI 2601 , PSCI 2602, or PSCI 3801 .

courses.name: Honours Seminar on a Selected Topic in Political Science
courses.description: A seminar on a selected contemporary topic in Political Science. Topic may vary from year to year and will be announced in advance of the registration period by the Department of Political Science.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: International Security and Terrorism
courses.description: Conventional approaches to international security; international security in the post-Cold War era; theories and debates on terrorism, its causes and types, and its impact on contemporary global security.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: International Politics of Forced Migration
courses.description: The relationship between international politics and the causes, consequences and responses to forced migration, internal displacement and refugees. Seminars and case studies are used to examine the evolution of the global refugee regime and the challenges it faces today.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Latin America and the World
courses.description: Latin America's changing relations with states, international institutions and non-state actors in the Global North and South.  Topics may include security, South-South cooperation, trade, investment and transnational migration and drug trafficking.
courses.prerequisites: fourth year standing or permission from the Department.

courses.name: Tutorial in a Selected Field
courses.description: Tutorials or reading courses on selected topics in which seminars are not available.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the Department and agreement of an instructor.

courses.name: Tutorial in a Selected Field
courses.description: Tutorials or reading courses on selected topics in which seminars are not available.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the Department and agreement of an instructor.

courses.name: Washington Center Seminar I
courses.description: A seminar offered by The Washington Center, governed by Carleton regulations, and co-ordinated by Carleton's Department of Political Science.
courses.prerequisites: selection to The Washington Center Internship Program and one of PSCI 2200 , PSCI 3200 , or PSCI 3210 .

courses.name: Washington Center Seminar II
courses.description: A seminar offered by The Washington Center, governed by Carleton regulations, and co-ordinated by Carleton's Department of Political Science.
courses.prerequisites: selection to The Washington Center Internship Program and one of PSCI 2200 , PSCI 3200 , or PSCI 3210 .

courses.name: Honours  Research Essay
courses.description: Individual research project resulting in a major essay, completed under the supervision of a faculty member and evaluated by both the supervisor and an appointed reader.  Students are responsible for locating a faculty member willing to supervise the essay. Departmental regulations apply.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing in Political Science with a Political Science CGPA of 9.00 or better, or permission of the Supervisor of Undergraduate Studies.
